name: Neue Campton
classification_notes: Neue Campton is a geometric sans-serif with clean, circular forms and a rational structure typical of the geometric tradition, with alternate characters offering humanist touches.
summary: A nine-weight geometric sans from René Bieder with full italic companions and a broad set of OpenType alternates for a/y/t/G/M.
description: Spanning nine weights from Thin to Black, each with a matching italic, Neue Campton covers a wide range suitable for editorial and branding work. OpenType features include oldstyle figures, tabular and proportional figures, fractions, case-sensitive forms and alternate glyphs for several characters including a, y, t, G and M. The geometric structure is clean and consistent without being especially distinctive, making it a reliable workhorse rather than a characterful choice.
foundry_blurb: Studio René Bieder presents Neue Campton as a versatile geometric sans-serif available in nine weights with italics, featuring an extensive set of OpenType features including figure variants, fractions, case-sensitive forms and multiple character alternates.

name: Neue Faktum
classification_notes: The clean, rational letterforms and alternate single-storey 'a' and 'g' options point to a geometric sans tradition, typical of contemporary neutral grotesques with geometric underpinnings.
summary: A clean geometric sans in eight weights with matching italics, offering a range of OpenType alternates for single-storey and alternate letterforms.
description: Running from Thin to ExtraBold with italic counterparts across all weights, the family covers broad weight range for both text and display work. A generous set of OpenType features includes alternate forms for 'a', 'g', 'y', 'J', 'G', 'Q' and arrows, alongside tabular and proportional figures, fractions and case-sensitive forms. The result is a workmanlike geometric sans suited to branding, editorial and UI applications where a neutral but considered voice is required.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Neue Faktum as a geometric sans-serif spanning eight weights from Thin to ExtraBold, each with an accompanying italic. The family includes a comprehensive set of OpenType features such as alternate letterforms, tabular and proportional figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures and case-sensitive forms.

name: Neue Faktum Condensed
classification_notes: A condensed grotesque sans-serif with alternates for single- and double-storey letterforms, placing it firmly in the grotesque tradition with contemporary refinements.
summary: A condensed grotesque spanning eight weights with matching italics, offering a thorough set of OpenType alternates including single- and double-storey options for a, g and y.
description: Neue Faktum Condensed covers eight weights from Thin to ExtraBold, each with a matching italic, giving 16 styles in a narrow cut suited to headlines, signage and space-constrained layouts. OpenType features include tabular and proportional figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures, case-sensitive forms and a broad range of character alternates for a, g, y, J, G, Q and arrows. The alternate glyph set provides meaningful typographic control for branding and editorial contexts.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder offers Neue Faktum Condensed as a 16-style condensed sans family ranging from Thin to ExtraBold with italics throughout, supported by an extensive OpenType feature set including figure styles, fractions, ligatures and multiple character alternates.

name: Neue Faktum Extended
classification_notes: An extended-width grotesque sans with geometric influences, offering a wide range of weights with matching italics and multiple OpenType alternates for single-storey and double-storey letterforms.
summary: An extended-width grotesque with eight weights, matching italics and a suite of OpenType alternates for letterform flexibility.
description: Running from Thin to ExtraBold with corresponding italics, this extended-width grotesque offers 16 styles suited to branding, editorial and display work. OpenType features include alternate single-storey and double-storey forms for a, g and y, case-sensitive forms, tabular and proportional figures, and fractions. The wider proportions give it a spacious, open quality at display sizes.
foundry_blurb: Neue Faktum Extended is an extended-width grotesque sans from Studio Rene Bieder, available in eight weights from Thin to ExtraBold with matching italics. The family includes a range of OpenType features including alternate letterforms for a, g, y, J, G and Q, as well as arrows, fractions, ligatures and both tabular and proportional figures.

name: Neue Freigeist
classification_notes: Neue Freigeist presents humanist sans-serif characteristics with a broad weight range and extensive OpenType alternates suggesting warmth and flexibility. The presence of alternate a, g, r and y forms points to a humanist rather than geometric or grotesque origin.
summary: A nine-weight humanist sans from Studio Rene Bieder with matching italics and a generous set of OpenType alternates across each style.
description: Spanning ExtraLight through Heavy with italics throughout, the family offers broad tonal range for both text and display use. A rich OpenType feature set includes alternate forms for a, g, r, y, Q, R and ß, alongside tabular and proportional figures, fractions and discretionary ligatures. Individual styles are sold separately at €35 each, making it a modular purchase rather than a single bundle.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Neue Freigeist as a wide-ranging sans-serif family with eighteen styles running from ExtraLight to Heavy, each with matching italics. The family features extensive OpenType functionality including multiple character alternates, case-sensitive forms and figure options.

name: Neue Galano
classification_notes: Clean, even stroke contrast and a wide weight range from ExtraLight to Heavy suggest a geometric sans construction; the alternate single-storey 'a' and other OpenType alternates reinforce a modern geometric character.
summary: A geometric sans spanning nine weights with matching italics and a set of OpenType alternates including single-storey 'a' and alternate arrows.
description: Neue Galano covers ExtraLight through Heavy in 18 styles, each paired with an italic, giving it a broad range for both text and display use. OpenType features include tabular and proportional figures, fractions, case-sensitive forms and several character alternates for 'a', 'y', 'M' and 'J'. The family sits comfortably in branding, editorial and interface contexts where a clean geometric sans is required without strong stylistic idiosyncrasy.
foundry_blurb: Studio René Bieder presents Neue Galano as a geometric sans-serif available in nine weights from ExtraLight to Heavy, each with a matching italic. The family includes a range of OpenType features such as alternate letterforms, tabular and proportional figures, fractions and case-sensitive forms, making it suitable for a wide variety of typographic applications.

name: Kreol Display
classification_notes: Kreol Display presents as a humanist sans-serif intended for display-scale use, with optical sizing positioning it alongside companion Text and Standard subfamilies within the broader Kreol family.
summary: A display-optimised humanist sans forming the largest optical size in Studio René Bieder's three-part Kreol family.
description: Kreol Display sits at the top of a three-member optical size system alongside Kreol Standard and Kreol Text, offering fourteen styles across seven weights from Thin to ExtraBold with matching italics. The character set includes small capitals, oldstyle figures, case-sensitive forms and a range of alternate glyphs for a, g, y, J, Q, R and arrows. The feature set makes it practical for editorial and identity work at headline scale without relying solely on weight contrast.
foundry_blurb: Kreol Display is the display-size member of the Kreol family from Studio René Bieder, available in fourteen styles from Thin to ExtraBold with italics throughout. The family includes extensive OpenType support with alternates for multiple characters, small capitals, fractions and oldstyle figures, and is accompanied by Kreol Standard and Kreol Text for multi-scale typographic systems.

name: Kreol Standard
classification_notes: The upright, clean construction and range of weights from Thin to ExtraBold with alternates for single-storey 'a' and 'g' point to a geometric sans tradition with some humanist detailing.
summary: A geometric sans with seven weights and matching italics, sitting within a three-subfamily system that spans display, standard and text optical roles.
description: Kreol Standard occupies the middle register of the Kreol family, positioned between the display and text cuts. Fourteen styles run from Thin to ExtraBold with italics throughout, and a generous set of OpenType features includes small capitals, oldstyle figures, case-sensitive forms and multiple alternates for characters such as 'a', 'g', 'J', 'Q' and 'R'. The alternate glyph set gives designers control over tone, shifting the face between a strict geometric and a slightly warmer register.
foundry_blurb: Kreol Standard is part of Studio Rene Bieder's Kreol family, which also includes Kreol Display and Kreol Text. The Standard cut offers 14 styles across 7 weights with italic counterparts, supported by an extensive OpenType feature set including small caps, oldstyle and tabular figures, fractions and a range of character alternates.

name: Kreol Text
classification_notes: The name and sibling families (Kreol Standard, Kreol Display) suggest a multi-optical-size serif system; the text cut is optimised for smaller sizes with features typical of contemporary text serifs.
summary: A text-optimised serif in six weights with italics, forming part of the three-cut Kreol family alongside Standard and Display variants.
description: Kreol Text sits at the smaller end of a three-member optical size system, joined by Kreol Standard and Kreol Display. Its twelve styles run from Light to ExtraBold with matching italics, and OpenType features include small capitals, oldstyle figures, fractions and a range of character alternates for a, g, y and several other letters. The overall system points toward editorial and publishing use where consistent performance across body text and display settings is required.
foundry_blurb: Kreol Text is the text-optimised cut of the Kreol family, available in 14 styles across six weights with italics. It ships with a full OpenType feature set including small capitals, oldstyle figures, case-sensitive forms and multiple character alternates.

name: Neue Magnat Display
classification_notes: The page explicitly labels the family as humanistic, and the range of alternate characters including double-storey a and g variants points to a humanist sans construction optimised for display use.
summary: A display-optimised humanist sans in six weights with matching italics, offering extensive OpenType alternates for expressive headline work.
description: Part of the wider Neue Magnat family alongside Standard and Text cuts, this display variant carries six weights from Light to ExtraBold, each with a matching italic. A generous set of OpenType alternates covers single and double-storey a and g, alternate k, y, ampersand, German umlauts, punctuation and arrows, giving designers fine control over character. Suited to editorial headlines, packaging and branding where a warm sans with personality is needed at large sizes.
foundry_blurb: Neue Magnat Display is the headline member of the Neue Magnat type system, which also includes Standard and Text optical sizes. The family spans twelve styles and features a broad range of OpenType features including tabular and proportional figures, lining figures, fractions, case-sensitive forms and numerous character alternates.

name: Neue Magnat Standard
classification_notes: Neue Magnat Standard sits within the humanist sans tradition, offering optical-size-specific text performance with warmth in its letterforms and a range of alternate characters suggesting expressive flexibility.
summary: A mid-range optical cut from Studio Rene Bieder's Neue Magnat family, offering six weights with italics and a generous set of OpenType alternates.
description: Part of a three-subfamily system alongside Display and Text cuts, this standard variant covers six weights from Light to ExtraBold, each with a matching italic. Alternate forms for a, g, y, k and the ampersand, plus case-sensitive forms and both oldstyle and lining figure sets, give compositors considerable fine-tuning options. Best suited to editorial and branding work where a clean humanist sans is needed with some room for character.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Neue Magnat Standard as part of the wider Neue Magnat family, which spans Display, Standard and Text subfamilies. The Standard cut is available in 12 styles and is priced per style at €35, with an extensive set of OpenType features including figure variants, fractions, case-sensitive forms and a range of character alternates.

name: Neue Magnat Text
classification_notes: Neue Magnat Text is a humanist sans-serif optimised for text sizes, featuring alternate single-storey and double-storey letterforms that suggest a warm, contemporary approach to the grotesque tradition.
summary: A text-weight cut of the Neue Magnat family, offering six weights with italics and a generous set of OpenType alternates for refined typesetting.
description: Part of a three-subfamily system alongside Display and Standard variants, the Text cut is shaped for sustained reading with optical calibration suited to smaller sizes. Six weights from Light to ExtraBold each carry a matching italic, while OpenType features include alternate forms for a, g, y, k and the ampersand, plus case-sensitive forms, tabular figures and fraction support. The alternate ÄÖÜ and punctuation options give German-language work additional typographic control.
foundry_blurb: Neue Magnat Text is part of Studio Rene Bieder's Neue Magnat family, which spans three sub-families: Text, Standard and Display, each available in 12 styles. The Text cut features six weights from Light to ExtraBold with matching italics, and includes an extensive set of OpenType features including multiple alternate glyphs, figure styles and case-sensitive forms.

name: Multima
classification_notes: Multima presents clean, geometric proportions with a wide weight range and alternate single-storey characters, placing it firmly in the geometric sans tradition. The availability of alternate forms for 'a', 'g' and 'y' signals influence from humanist geometry hybrids.
summary: A geometric sans in ten weights with matching italics, offering a broad range of OpenType alternates for single and double-storey character variants.
description: Running from Thin through to an Ultra weight, Multima covers an unusually wide tonal range within a single family, with full italic companions throughout. OpenType features include tabular figures, fractions, case-sensitive forms and a set of alternates covering 'a', 'g', 'y', 't', 'ß', 'G', '3' and directional arrows, giving designers meaningful control over personality. The result is a workhorse geometric suitable for both text and display use across branding and editorial contexts.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Multima as a geometric sans spanning ten weights from Thin to Ultra, each with a corresponding italic. The family ships with a full suite of OpenType features including tabular figures, fractions, case-sensitive forms and multiple alternate characters, allowing users to toggle between distinct stylistic interpretations of key letterforms.

name: Neurath
classification_notes: Neurath presents as a geometric sans with clean, rationalist construction and a broad weight range typical of the genre. The alternate character set including single-storey 'a' and alternate 'g' and 'y' reinforces its geometric lineage.
summary: A geometric sans in nine weights with matching italics, offering a broad suite of OpenType alternates including multiple single-character variants and case-sensitive forms.
description: Spanning Thin to Black with full italic companions across all eighteen styles, Neurath is built for versatility in editorial and corporate contexts. OpenType features include tabular figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures, case-sensitive forms and a generous set of alternates covering 'a', 'y', 't', 'ß', 'G', arrows and the ampersand. The clean, rationalist construction places it firmly in the mid-European geometric sans tradition, with enough character variation to avoid blankness.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Neurath as a geometric sans-serif available in nine weights from Thin to Black, each with an italic, sold at €35 per style. The family includes a rich set of OpenType features such as tabular figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures, case-sensitive forms and multiple character alternates.

name: Nylo
classification_notes: Nylo presents clean geometric forms with single-storey alternate glyphs available via OpenType features, typical of contemporary geometric sans families designed for versatility across weight ranges.
summary: A geometric sans with ten weights, matching italics and a generous set of OpenType alternates covering single and double-storey letterform variants.
description: Twenty styles run from Thin to Heavy, each with a matching italic, giving the family considerable range for both text and display use. OpenType features include alternate forms for a, g, y, l, t and M, alongside oldstyle and tabular figures, fractions and an alternate ampersand, making it adaptable to different typographic registers. The alternates allow the same typeface to shift between a strict geometric personality and a softer, more humanist one.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Nylo as a wide-ranging sans offered in ten weights with italics throughout. Each style is sold individually at €35 and comes with an extensive suite of OpenType features including case-sensitive forms, alternate letterforms, oldstyle figures, tabular figures and fractions.

name: Quaria Display
classification_notes: Named as a display typeface by the foundry itself, with a companion text family (Quaria Text) suggesting optical size differentiation. The styling, with numerous alternates for distinctive characters, points to a display-oriented serif.
summary: A display serif from René Bieder offered in five weights with matching italics and an extensive suite of OpenType alternates for key characters.
description: Quaria Display sits at the heavier end of a two-family system alongside Quaria Text, with the display cut optimised for larger settings. Five weights from Regular to ExtraBold each carry an italic, and the OpenType feature set includes small capitals, oldstyle figures, fractions and individual alternates for characters including a, g, y, Q and the ampersand. The breadth of alternates gives designers meaningful control over personality at headline sizes.
foundry_blurb: Studio René Bieder presents Quaria Display as part of a wider Quaria system, with a companion text family available separately. The display family offers ten styles and a rich set of OpenType features including multiple character alternates, small capitals and figure variants.

name: Quaria Text
classification_notes: The page presents a text-optimised serif family with optical sizing distinct from its Display sibling, featuring humanist characteristics and a full range of OpenType features typical of contemporary text serifs.
summary: A text-optimised serif in five weights with matching italics, offering an extensive set of OpenType alternates suited to editorial and publishing work.
description: Quaria Text spans Regular to ExtraBold across ten styles, each weight paired with an italic. The family ships with small capitals, oldstyle figures, tabular figures, fractions and a broad range of character alternates covering glyphs such as a, g, y, J, Q, G and R/K/k. A Display sibling handles larger sizes, making the two together a practical pairing for structured typographic systems.
foundry_blurb: Studio René Bieder presents Quaria Text as the text-optimised member of the Quaria family, available in ten styles from Regular to ExtraBold Italic, with an accompanying Display variant sold separately. The family includes extensive OpenType support with multiple alternate characters and figure styles.

name: Neue Rational Condensed
classification_notes: A condensed grotesque sans-serif with a broad weight range and alternate character forms, consistent with the neo-grotesque tradition adapted for narrow measures.
summary: A condensed grotesque spanning nine weights with matching italics and a suite of OpenType alternates for flexible typesetting.
description: Neue Rational Condensed runs from Thin to Black across eighteen styles, each weight paired with an italic. OpenType features include tabular and proportional figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures, case-sensitive forms and multiple alternate glyphs for a, g, y and t. The condensed proportions make it well suited to tight editorial layouts, data-heavy interfaces and headline work where horizontal space is at a premium.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Neue Rational Condensed as a narrow grotesque family available across nine weights with italics, featuring an extensive set of OpenType alternates including alternate letterforms for a, g, y and t, alongside figure variants, fractions and case-sensitive forms.

name: Neue Rational Narrow
classification_notes: A narrow-cut geometric sans with clean, rational letterforms and extensive OpenType alternates, fitting squarely in the geometric sans tradition with a condensed width orientation.
summary: A narrow geometric sans in nine weights with matching italics, offering a range of OpenType alternates for letterform customisation.
description: Spanning Thin to Black with italic companions at every weight, this condensed geometric sans is built for space-efficient typesetting without sacrificing character. OpenType features include tabular and proportional figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures and case-sensitive forms, alongside character alternates for a, g, y and t. The narrow width makes it well suited to editorial layouts, data-heavy interfaces and branding work where column economy matters.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Neue Rational Narrow as a condensed companion offering nine weights from Thin to Black, each with a matching italic, and a full suite of OpenType features including multiple character alternates and figure styles.

name: Neue Rational
classification_notes: The name and page copy reference rationalism directly, and the upright geometric construction with alternate single-storey 'a' and 'g' forms is consistent with a geometric sans tradition.
summary: A nine-weight geometric sans with matching italics and a thorough set of OpenType alternates for publication and branding work.
description: Spanning nine weights from Thin to Black, each with an italic, the family offers broad typographic range for editorial and corporate applications. OpenType features include tabular and proportional figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures and alternates for 'a', 'g', 'y' and 't', giving designers granular control over texture and voice. The alternate single-storey forms allow the family to shift between a strict rational register and something slightly warmer.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Neue Rational as a geometric sans family available across nine weights with full italic companions, priced per style. The family includes an extensive OpenType feature set covering figure variants, fractions, ligatures, case-sensitive forms and multiple character alternates including arrows.

name: Neue Rational Mix
classification_notes: Neue Rational Mix is a geometric sans-serif in the Neue Rational family, offering alternate letterform options that suggest a hybrid between strict geometric and humanist influences, typical of Bieder's rational design approach.
summary: A geometric sans from the Neue Rational family offering eight styles and a range of alternate letterforms via OpenType features.
description: Part of Studio Rene Bieder's Neue Rational system, this mix variant spans Regular through Bold with matching italics and a set of OpenType alternates covering the a, g, y and t. Features include tabular and proportional figures, case-sensitive forms, fractions and alternate arrows, giving designers meaningful typographic control within a clean geometric framework. Related to both the core Neue Rational and the Neue Rational Mono, it sits comfortably in editorial, interface and brand work.
foundry_blurb: Neue Rational Mix is part of the broader Neue Rational family from Studio Rene Bieder, available in eight styles across four weights. It includes a rich set of OpenType features such as tabular figures, fractions, case-sensitive forms and multiple single-character alternates, and is sold alongside the related Neue Rational and Neue Rational Mono.

name: Neue Rational Mono
classification_notes: The typeface is explicitly monospaced, related to the Neue Rational sans-serif family, with geometric proportions and fixed-width spacing throughout all weights.
summary: A fixed-width companion to the Neue Rational family, offered in four weights with italics and a generous set of OpenType alternates.
description: Part of Studio Rene Bieder's Neue Rational system, this monospaced variant runs from Regular to Bold with matching italics across eight styles. OpenType features include case-sensitive forms, discretionary ligatures, fractions and multiple single-character alternates for a, g, y and t, giving the designer room to adjust the texture of the face. Sits alongside Neue Rational and Neue Rational Mix as a technical companion for screen, editorial and data-heavy settings.
foundry_blurb: Neue Rational Mono is presented as part of a broader type system alongside Neue Rational and Neue Rational Mix, available in eight styles from Regular to Bold Italic, each sold individually at €35.

name: Spyk Display
classification_notes: The display-oriented cut of a geometric sans family, with a wide weight range and alternate letterforms suggesting a clean, constructed aesthetic suited to headline use.
summary: A display-weight geometric sans spanning seven weights with matching italics and a rich set of OpenType alternates for headline and editorial use.
description: Spyk Display runs from Thin to Black across 14 styles, each weight paired with an italic. The OpenType feature set is thorough, offering small capitals, oldstyle figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures, case-sensitive forms and multiple character alternates for a, g, y, K, R, the ampersand and arrows. A companion text cut, Spyk Text, extends the family for smaller sizes.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder presents Spyk Display as a 14-style geometric sans with a full weight range from Thin to Black, matched italics throughout, and an extensive suite of OpenType features including small capitals, oldstyle figures, alternate letterforms and arrow variants.

name: Spyk Text
classification_notes: The page groups Spyk Text alongside Spyk Display, suggesting a text-optimised grotesque sans with a companion display family. The range of alternates for single-storey a and g point to a humanist-influenced grotesque.
summary: A text-optimised grotesque from Studio Rene Bieder, running six weights with italics and a thorough set of OpenType alternates.
description: Covering six weights from Light to Black, each with a matching italic, the family is built around text performance rather than display drama. OpenType features include small capitals, oldstyle figures, tabular figures, fractions and a range of character alternates for a, g, y, K and R, giving designers reasonable flexibility in shaping tone. A companion Spyk Display family handles the heavier-duty headline work.
foundry_blurb: Studio Rene Bieder present Spyk Text as a 12-style text family with an extensive OpenType feature set including small capitals, oldstyle and tabular figures, fractions, discretionary ligatures and multiple character alternates, sold alongside the companion Spyk Display.

name: Stakkat
classification_notes: The page shows a clean, upright sans-serif with alternate single-storey 'a' and 'g' forms, suggesting a humanist or neo-grotesque construction typical of contemporary Swiss-influenced design.
summary: A six-weight sans-serif with matching italics from Studio René Bieder, offering a range of OpenType alternates including single-storey 'a' and 'g' variants.
description: Twelve styles run from Light to ExtraBold, each paired with an italic, giving solid weight coverage for both text and display work. OpenType features include small capitals, oldstyle figures, fractions, tabular figures and case-sensitive forms, alongside character alternates for 'a', 'g', 'y', arrows and an alternate ampersand in some styles. The feature set suggests a family designed with editorial and branding versatility in mind.
foundry_blurb: Studio René Bieder presents Stakkat as a contemporary sans-serif available in 12 styles across six weights, with comprehensive OpenType support including small capitals, figure variants and multiple character alternates.

name: Vitruv Poster
classification_notes: A high-contrast serif designed explicitly for poster and display use, with classical Roman naming conventions suggesting an antique or neoclassical stylistic lineage suited to large-scale settings.
summary: A high-contrast display serif from René Bieder's Vitruv family, optimised for poster-scale settings with classical Roman-themed style names.
description: Part of the broader Vitruv type system alongside Text and Headline subfamilies, the Poster cut is built for large sizes where contrast and presence matter most. Twelve styles span Thin to Black with matching italics, and the family carries a substantial OpenType feature set including small capitals, oldstyle figures, tabular figures, case-sensitive forms and several alternate glyphs. Style names drawn from Roman history and architecture — Germanicus, Romanum, Augustus, Alexandria — signal an antique classical reference that runs through the design.
foundry_blurb: Vitruv Poster is the display-optimised member of the Vitruv family, offered in twelve styles from Thin to Black Italic. Each style is individually named after Roman figures and terms, and the family ships with a broad set of OpenType features including small capitals, oldstyle and tabular figures, fractions, and multiple alternates.

name: Vitruv Headline
classification_notes: The Roman-themed naming conventions and headline orientation suggest a classical serif with transitional proportions, designed for display use at larger sizes within a three-part optical size system.
summary: A classically inspired headline serif from René Bieder, part of a three-family optical size system spanning poster, headline and text cuts.
description: Vitruv Headline sits in the middle tier of a Roman-themed type system that includes Vitruv Poster and Vitruv Text, each optimised for different size ranges. The family runs across six weights from Thin to Black, all with matching italics, giving twelve styles in total. The naming of individual styles after figures and concepts of antiquity — Augustus, Alexandria, Romanum — reinforces the classical framing of the design.
foundry_blurb: Studio René Bieder presents Vitruv Headline as part of the wider Vitruv family, which spans three optical-size cuts: Poster, Headline and Text. The Headline cut offers twelve styles across six weights with italics throughout, each style named after Roman figures and concepts.

name: Vitruv Text
classification_notes: The Roman naming conventions, classical proportions and range from Thin to Black with matching italics suggest a text-optimised transitional or classical serif intended for editorial and body copy use.
summary: A classical text serif in six weights with matching italics, built around Roman naming conventions and a thorough set of OpenType features for editorial and publishing work.
description: Twelve styles run from Thin to Black with corresponding italics, each named after a figure or concept from Roman antiquity. OpenType features include small capitals, oldstyle and tabular figures, fractions, case-sensitive forms and several character alternates, making it well equipped for long-form setting. Part of a three-subfamily system alongside Vitruv Headline and Vitruv Poster, allowing consistent use across hierarchies.
foundry_blurb: Vitruv Text is part of the broader Vitruv type system from Studio Rene Bieder, which spans Text, Headline and Poster variants. The Text subfamily offers 12 styles from Thin to Black with italics throughout, and includes an extensive range of OpenType features such as small capitals, oldstyle figures, tabular figures, fractions, case-sensitive forms and multiple character alternates.
